response.justification: Maria Rodriguez fails to meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet her resume shows only a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months) and a bachelor's thesis project using C++, totaling well under one year of professional C++ exposure. Her primary expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang in web and mobile contexts, which is fundamentally misaligned with a C++-driven cloud compute platform leadership role. While she demonstrates solid engineering practices and some distributed systems exposure, the technical stack mismatch and absence of cloud platform leadership experience make her an unsuitable candidate.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture including TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and code quality practices demonstrated across multiple roles
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O requirements
Active community presence through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects indicating communication and mentoring capability
concerns: Critical C++ experience deficit — the role mandates 8 years minimum; the resume shows approximately 6 months of professional C++ use
No evidence of cloud compute platform architecture or technical leadership at infrastructure scale; AWS CDK use was limited to a pricing team context
Technical identity is firmly rooted in full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not in systems programming or cloud infrastructure engineering
